claims.check: The saved checkpoint is readable, scoped to dedicated-leaf method calibration, and pinned for this verification
claims.threshold: checkpoint exists with calibration-only decision boundary and a recorded sha256
evidence: The checkpoint limits its decision to a Node 26 dedicated-leaf allocation-sampling method.
The checkpoint blocks pumped-fn allocation, retained-memory, regression, and optimization claims.

claims.check: Runtime, leaf, workload, sampler, observation count, and acceptance gates were frozen before observation
claims.threshold: predeclare and scripts predate all raw profiles; one observation per control; zero maximum=0; ratio=1.8..2.2; signal-to-noise minimum=10
claims.value: predeclare/scripts mtime=2026-07-16T05:11:50Z; raw mtimes=2026-07-16T05:12:03Z; all declared controls and thresholds matched
claims.replay: Read predeclare.json and compare its controls, thresholds, and file timestamps with observe.mjs, replay.mjs, and the raw profiles
evidence: Node.js 26.4.0; V8 14.6.202.34-node.21; allocation leaf=allocateOne.
batchObjects=200000; samplingIntervalBytes=32768; stackDepth=64; includeObjectsCollectedByMajorGC=false.
observationsPerControl=1 for zero, one, and two.
zeroAttributedBytesMax=0; oneAttributedBytesMinExclusive=0; twoToOneRatio=1.8..2.2; signalToNoiseMin=10.

claims.check: Every evidence hash cited by the checkpoint matches the saved artifact bytes
claims.replay: sha256sum the seven cited allocation-leaf artifacts and compare with checkpoint evidence_refs_or_hashes
evidence: All seven checkpoint evidence references matched their saved files exactly.

claims.check: The saved checker reproduces the exact zero, one, two, ratio, and signal-to-noise values
claims.threshold: zero=0; one=12882032; two=25534256; ratio=1.9821605783932223 within 1.8..2.2; signal-to-noise=12882032 >=10; methodAccepted=true
claims.value: zero=0; one=12882032; two=25534256; ratio=1.9821605783932223; signal-to-noise=12882032; methodAccepted=true
claims.replay: cd /tmp/dkr-p4-allocation-leaf && node replay.mjs raw-zero.json raw-one.json raw-two.json
evidence: observations=[zero:0, one:12882032, two:25534256]
twoToOneRatio=1.9821605783932223
signalToNoise=12882032
checks={zeroExact:true,onePositive:true,ratio:true,signalToNoise:true}
methodAccepted=true

claims.check: Each profile uses the frozen runtime, sampler settings, batch count, and retained in-heap control
claims.threshold: metadata exactly matches Node 26.4.0, V8 14.6.202.34-node.21, 200000 objects per batch, interval 32768, depth 64, major-GC collection false, and retainedBatchCount equal to 0, 1, and 2
claims.value: all three profile metadata records matched; retainedBatchCount=[0,1,2]
claims.replay: Read the observer retention path and compare all raw metadata through replay.mjs metadata guards
evidence: observe.mjs pushes each 200000-object batch into the process-local retained array before profiling stops.
Raw metadata reports batches and retainedBatchCount as zero=0, one=1, two=2.
The one-shot replay rejects any runtime, V8, sampler, batch, or retention metadata drift.

claims.check: The saved study evidence contains one frozen output per control and the observer prevents overwriting those outputs
claims.threshold: exactly one named raw profile for each control; output-exists guard; repeats_after_result=0
claims.value: raw-zero.json, raw-one.json, and raw-two.json exist once; observe.mjs exits 2 when the requested output exists; finish record reports repeats_after_result=0
claims.replay: Read observe.mjs output-exists guard, enumerate the frozen raw profile names, and compare progress.finish.json observation_count
evidence: The frozen directory contains one raw-zero.json, one raw-one.json, and one raw-two.json.
observe.mjs exits before sampling when its output path already exists.
progress.finish.json records one observation per control and zero repeats after result.
This verifies the saved study boundary; it does not claim visibility into observations outside the frozen directory.

claims.check: The checkpoint remains within its 30 minute freshness window
claims.replay: Read observed_at and max_age and compare with date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ
evidence: checkpoint observed_at=2026-07-16T05:12:03Z
validator observed_at=2026-07-16T05:19:16Z
max_age=30m

claims.check: Only the calibration method may advance as reducing discovery; product allocation, retained-memory, regression, and optimization claims remain blocked
claims.threshold: method calibration supported under frozen controls; no pumped-fn allocation or optimization claim
claims.value: dedicated-leaf method calibration accepted for reducing discovery only; product claims remain blocked
claims.replay: Compare the replay decision with checkpoint questions_unanswered, downstream_decision, calibration_boundary, and wall_gate
evidence: The replay accepts the dedicated allocateOne leaf under the frozen Node 26 controls.
No pumped-fn workload was measured and no baseline-versus-candidate allocation comparison exists.
The probe does not measure retained heap after cleanup.
CKR performance, product allocation claims, retained-memory claims, and optimization remain blocked.

wall_gate.reason: The dedicated-leaf calibration replay is valid under its frozen Node 26 controls, but no pumped-fn workload, retained-memory outcome, or baseline-versus-candidate allocation comparison was measured.
